elements.sent-2411-001: In this article we identify a sharp ill-posedness/well-posedness threshold for kinetic wave equations (KWE) derived from quasilinear Schr\"{o dinger models.}
elements.sent-2411-002: Our analysis shows that KWE becomes ill-posed in a range where the original quasilinear equation is itself well-posed.
elements.sent-2411-003: We also prove that both the gain-only and full equation share the same well-posedness threshold, thus legitimizing a gain-only approach to solving 4-wave kinetic equations.
elements.sent-2411-004: Kinetic equations are widely used to approximate complicated systems of many interacting agents (particles, waves,...) that cannot directly be simulated numerically due to their immense size.
elements.sent-2411-005: The field of derivation of such equations for weakly nonlinearly interacting waves has seen impressive advances recently: after a series of works on the rigorous derivation of the 4-wave kinetic wave equation (KWE) from the cubic nonlinear Schr\'odinger equation (NLS) see e.g. \cite{BGHS,CG1,CG2,DH1 , the state of the art result was obtained by Z.. Hani and Y.}
elements.sent-2411-006: Deng \cite{DH2 : they derived the equation up to the kinetic time and later showed validity of the (KWE) for as long as it is well-posed \cite{DH4 .}}
elements.sent-2411-007: Recently, the same authors along with X.
elements.sent-2411-008: Ma \cite{DHM extended their program to interacting particles and provided a long time derivation of the Boltzmann equation, again subject to the condition of well-posedness.}
elements.sent-2411-009: Besides the cubic (NLS), other models such as KdV-type \cite{ST,Ma or stochastic KP equations \cite{Faou have also been considered to derive 3-wave kinetic equations.}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Spatially inhomogeneous turbulence has also been studied and effective transport equations have been derived in \cite{AmCoGer, HaRoStTr, HaShZh .}
elements.sent-2411-011: However, despite the promising results in the field of derivation, the understanding of the kinetic equations themselves remains poor.
elements.sent-2411-012: Yet this is a crucial step in the derivation program since all such results are conditional on existence of a smooth solution to the corresponding kinetic equation.
elements.sent-2411-013: In this paper we seek to make some progress in this direction, and address local well-posedness for a family of kinetic wave equations derived from quasilinear Schr\"{o dinger systems.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Regarding the choice of the model, we are motivated by the fact that derivation of kinetic equations from quasilinear systems seems challenging.
elements.sent-2411-015: The results available are less convincing than for semilinear equations (meaning their validity is proved on much smaller time scales).
elements.sent-2411-016: A possible explanation is that kinetic theory does not apply as well to quasilinear equations, which would manifest as ill-posedness of formally derived corresponding kinetic approximations.
elements.sent-2411-017: The choice of the equations here allows us to correlate precisely the amount of derivative loss (measured by the parameter $\beta$, see \eqref{MMT ) with the local well-posedness of the kinetic wave equation.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Our analysis identifies a sharp threshold of derivative loss for well-posedness of the equation to hold.
elements.sent-2411-019: That is, if the original equation loses more derivatives than that threshold, then its kinetic equation is ill-posed locally in time.
elements.sent-2411-020: Conversely if it loses less, then the kinetic equation is well-posed.
elements.sent-2411-021: Note that the original model of interacting waves remains well-posed in the entire quasilinear range.
elements.sent-2411-022: We provide possible explanations for the numerical value of the threshold, arguing both on mathematical and physical grounds.
elements.sent-2411-023: A second insight provided by our analysis is that the threshold is the same for the full equation and its gain-only counterpart.
elements.sent-2411-024: Interestingly however, ill-posedness is achieved in different ways for both equations.
elements.sent-2411-025: Indeed the full equation has some cancellations that are not quite strong enough to restore well-posedness, but do dampen low frequencies.
